articles.title: Plan to scrap presidential elections puts Zimbabweans at loggerheads
articles.description: The ruling party has unveiled a draft law that strips voters of the chance to elect the president.

articles.title: Beneficiation: Govt takes lead
articles.description: Debra Matabvu Herald Reporter GOVERNMENT has identified at least eight mining sites — mainly in the lithium and iron sectors — for designation as Special Economic Zones, in a major... The post Beneficiation: Govt takes lead appeared first on herald .

articles.title: Vic Falls allure spawns tourist rush
articles.description: Rutendo Nyeve-Bulawayo Bureau A RECORD number of local and international tourists are visiting the Victoria Falls as the waterfall reaches its peak, defying fears that numbers could drop due to... The post Vic Falls allure spawns tourist rush appeared first on herald .

more.title: How the Skeleton of Mutapa Times is Based on Sarah Charlesworth's Work
more.description: Conceptual artist Sarah Charlesworth's 1979 study of how newspapers covered a solar eclipse inspired the framework behind The Mutapa Times.

more.title: Zimbabwe’s Intellectual Paralysis: The Crisis of Descriptive Analysis, Failure on Prescriptive, Predictive and Imperative of Imagination
more.description: Zimbabwe’s contemporary intellectual and policy landscape is haunted by a profound inertia—an epistemic paralysis rooted in its overreliance on descriptive rather than prescriptive or predictive analysis. For decades, national discourse has remained tethered to the comfort of observation: dissecting what has happened and what is happening, but rarely daring to imagine what ought to happen […]The post Zimbabwe’s Intellectual Paralysis: The Crisis of Descriptive Analysis, Failure on Prescriptive, Predictive and Imperative of Imagination appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail.

more.title: Between Principle and Pragmatism: The Moral Fault Lines in Zimbabwe’s Constitutional Debate
more.description: Strip away the procedural arguments, the partisan loyalties, and the ritualised exchanges of constitutional interpretation, and the Zimbabwean debate over amendment reveals something older and more enduring than law. Beneath the legal vocabulary lies a contest between competing moral imaginations, different ways of understanding authority, time, obligation, and political possibility.Zimbabwe travel guide By Brighton Musonza […]The post Between Principle and Pragmatism: The Moral Fault Lines in Zimbabwe’s Constitutional Debate appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail.

more.title: World Bank downgrades sub-Saharan Africa growth projection amid geopolitical risks
more.description: The World Bank on Wednesday revised its 2026 economic growth projection for sub-Saharan Africa downward by 0.3 percentage points from the October 2025 forecast, citing geopolitical risks. According to the latest edition of the Africa Economic Update, sub-Saharan Africa’s economic recovery from a decade of global shocks is showing signs of stalling. While growth is […]

more.title: Rights body witnessed intimidation, whip-wielding vetters at CAB3 hearings
more.description: HARARE – Zimbabwe’s human rights watchdog has raised the alarm over widespread intimidation and harassment of opponents of Constitutional Amendment Bill No. 3 during parliamentary public hearings, including the use of men armed with whips to vet participants in Mashonaland West. The Zimbabwe Human Rights Commission (ZHRC) deployed monitoring teams across all provinces from March […]

more.title: Insisting on a referendum devoid of a legal basis, says AG
more.description: Attorney-General Mrs Virginia Mabiza has dismissed calls for a referendum on the proposed constitutional changes, saying the demands have no legal basis and are driven by political considerations rather than constitutional requirements. In an interview, Mrs Mabiza said the Constitution clearly defines the limited circumstances under which a referendum is required, and argued that the […]

more.title: Calls Grow for Referendum as Constitutional Amendment Bill Hearings Turn Contentious
more.description: Public hearings on Zimbabwe’s Constitutional Amendment Bill No. 3 have entered a critical phase, with growing calls from citizens and analysts for the proposed changes to be subjected to a national referendum. The Bill, currently before the Parliament of Zimbabwe, seeks to extend the terms of office for councillors, Members of Parliament and the President […]

more.title: Chinamasa under fire over ‘colonial sins’ remarks after Coltart assault
more.description: Patrick Chinamasa has ignited controversy after suggesting that human rights lawyer Douglas Coltart was made to “atone” for the alleged colonial legacy of his father following a violent incident in Harare. The remarks come after Coltart was reportedly assaulted by suspected ZANU-PF supporters during a public hearing for Constitutional Amendment Bill No. 3 at the […]

more.title: Charamba questions BBC framing of Zimbabwe Constitutional Amendment debate
more.description: Deputy Chief Secretary in the Office of the President and Cabinet (Presidential Communications), George Charamba has criticised a recent BBC report on Zimbabwe’s Constitutional Amendment No. 3 Bill, disputing its characterisation of the reforms as a “power grab.” The BBC story reportedly suggested that the ruling party, ZANU-PF, was seeking to consolidate power through constitutional […]

more.title: A new generation is reviving the iPod for distraction-free listening
more.description: Remember the iPod? It’s making a quiet comeback. Four years after Apple killed off its digital music player, secondhand sales are surging. It’s fueled in part by young people interested not just in its retro looks but a desire to listen to music in a focused way and with playlists not determined by algorithms. “There’s […]

more.title: Zimbabwe to introduce lithium export quotas, sets conditions for resumption of shipments
more.description: Zimbabwe will introduce lithium concentrate export quotas and require commitments for more local processing as part of conditions to allow the resumption of mineral exports, the mines ministry has told producers. Africa’s top lithium producer suspended exports of lithium concentrates and other unprocessed minerals on February 26, after the government alleged malpractices and leakages. In […]

more.title: Ceasefire in the Iran-Isreal-US war teeters on the brink
more.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates — A tentative ceasefire in the Iran war staggered Thursday under the weight of Israel’s intense bombardment of Beirut, Tehran’s continued chokehold on the Strait of Hormuz, and uncertainty over whether negotiators can find common ground on a range of other differences. Hours after the ceasefire was announced — amid disagreement […]

more.title: CAF president Motsepe calls for unity after AFCON final fracas
more.description: DAKAR, Senegal – Confederation of African Football (CAF) president Patrice Motsepe would welcome an investigation into corruption at the organisation, saying they have nothing to hide following a meeting with Senegalese officials in ​Dakar on Wednesday. Senegal’s government last month demanded an investigation into corruption after the ‌country was stripped of their 2025 Africa Cup […]

more.title: Oil prices rise again and Asian stocks retreat on the fragile Iran ceasefire
more.description: HONG KONG — Oil rose again to above $97 a barrel and Asian stocks were trading mostly lower Thursday on skepticism over a fragile ceasefire between the U.S. and Iran. Investors were closely watching whether a two-week ceasefire between the United States and Iran was already slipping after a round of intense Israeli strikes on […]

more.title: A Voice That Refused to Fade: Remembering Leonard MusoroWenyoka Dembo
more.description: By Dr Masimba Mavaza | On this day in 1996, Zimbabwe lost one of its greatest musical storytellers, Leonard Dembo, ... Continue reading "A Voice That Refused to Fade: Remembering Leonard MusoroWenyoka Dembo"

more.title: ‘Business absorbs Middle East war cost pressures to keep price steady’
more.description: INDUSTRY and Commerce Minister Mangaliso Ndlovu says the prices of most basic commodities in Zimbabwe have remained relatively stable despite a global surge in fuel costs triggered by the ongoing Middle East crisis. Speaking at a dialogue on geopolitical risks hosted by a regional economic think tank, Africa Economic Development Strategies (AEDS), at the Rainbow [...] The post ‘Business absorbs Middle East war cost pressures to keep price steady’ appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail .
